title: Deep-Cut Pop Culture
description: Harder entertainment trivia for film, music, TV, awards, and franchise fans.

questions.prompt: Which film won the first Academy Award for Best Animated Feature?
questions.hint: The category was first awarded for films released in 2001.
questions.explanation: 'Shrek' won the first Oscar for Best Animated Feature. For further study, review Academy Award category history.

questions.prompt: Which artist's real name was Prince Rogers Nelson?
questions.hint: His stage name was also his given first name.
questions.explanation: Prince was born Prince Rogers Nelson. For further study, explore the Minneapolis sound and Prince's discography.

questions.prompt: 'The Empire Strikes Back' was released before 'Return of the Jedi.'
questions.hint: The original trilogy's middle chapter came out in 1980.
questions.explanation: 'The Empire Strikes Back' was released in 1980, before 'Return of the Jedi' in 1983. For further study, review Star Wars release order versus story chronology.

questions.prompt: The fictional coffee shop in 'Friends' is Central _____.
questions.hint: The name is a coffee pun.
questions.explanation: The coffee shop is Central Perk. For further study, compare recurring sitcom locations and how they anchor ensemble shows.

questions.prompt: Which director made both 'Jaws' and 'E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial'?
questions.hint: He is also associated with Indiana Jones.
questions.explanation: Steven Spielberg directed both 'Jaws' and 'E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial.' For further study, trace Spielberg's blockbuster influence.

questions.prompt: Which novel introduced the character Katniss Everdeen?
questions.explanation: Katniss Everdeen is the protagonist of 'The Hunger Games.' For further study, review 2000s young adult dystopian fiction.

questions.prompt: Which band released the album 'OK Computer'?
questions.hint: The band is also known for 'Paranoid Android.'
questions.explanation: Radiohead released 'OK Computer' in 1997. For further study, explore 1990s alternative rock landmarks.

questions.prompt: The character Gollum appears in 'The Lord of the Rings.'
questions.hint: He is obsessed with the One Ring.
questions.explanation: Gollum is a major character in J.R.R. Tolkien's Middle-earth stories. For further study, review the role of the One Ring across the trilogy.

questions.prompt: Which actor played Jack Dawson in 'Titanic'?
questions.hint: He later won an Oscar for 'The Revenant.'
questions.explanation: Leonardo DiCaprio played Jack Dawson in 'Titanic.' For further study, compare late-1990s blockbuster star turns.

questions.prompt: Which TV series includes the fictional continent of Westeros?
questions.hint: It is based on George R.R. Martin's fantasy novels.
questions.explanation: Westeros is a major setting in 'Game of Thrones.' For further study, review fantasy worldbuilding in television adaptations.

questions.prompt: The fictional African nation in 'Black Panther' is _____.
questions.hint: It is technologically advanced and rich in vibranium.
questions.explanation: Wakanda is the fictional home of Black Panther. For further study, explore how superhero settings shape character identity.

questions.prompt: Which musical features the song 'My Shot'?
questions.explanation: 'My Shot' is from the musical 'Hamilton.' For further study, review modern Broadway cast albums and historical musicals.
